blocks.content: The remaining five instinctively realized the extent of Shagal's rapid killing skills.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Get ready.
They scattered around Ethella, each drawing their weapons and radiating a deadly intent.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: We are the same kind.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: The same kind?
Shagal's eyebrows twitched.
He did not consider himself superior to them, but even in killing, there were levels of skill.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: I don't mix with anyone. Put away that creepy talk and just come at me if you want to die.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: We used to think that way too.
A pot-bellied man without any hair on his head wound a steel cable around his palm as he approached.

blocks.speaker: Pot-bellied man
blocks.content: You might not know which era you're from, but this is hell. Here, people like us are law-abiding citizens. If you're just going to stand on your pathetic pride, you'll receive the baptism of the black monk.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Black monk?
The pot-bellied man laughed.

blocks.speaker: Pot-bellied man
blocks.content: Clearly, you know nothing. Let me teach you slowly about the horrors of hell. Have you ever heard of the strangler Edron?
Shagal remained indifferent, but Ethella looked at the man in shock.
Strangler Edron. A murderer who strangled 109 people to death brutally 180 years ago.
Edron tightened the steel cable.

blocks.speaker: Edron
blocks.content: It doesn't matter what it is. If it breathes, I can strangle it. Humans are the most fun. Their tongues stick out long, their eyes roll back. Their legs kick as they try to survive—it drives me crazy.
According to Ethella's memory, he had been imprisoned and hanged himself on the 44th day.

blocks.speaker: Edron
blocks.content: Human Cutter Averio.
A man with severe buck teeth manipulated a large cutter like scissors and said,

blocks.speaker: Averio
blocks.content: I killed 287 people. My hobby is disassembling. Engineering, biological functions—I once disassembled a human into 3,287 parts. That's my personal best. Might break that record today. Just so you know…
His lips curled up wickedly.

blocks.speaker: Averio
blocks.content: You don't die easily in hell.
The two murderers Shagal had stabbed were still writhing.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Clearly, we need information.
Ethella didn't want to help but felt bad being caught unaware.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: I'll give you a chance. Those who want to live, raise your hand. Just one, I'll spare one who can give me information.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Heh, knew it.
A weasel-like man took out a scythe and crouched like a rat.

blocks.speaker: Weasel-like man
blocks.content: Wherever you scraped by, senior treatment here is really messed up. Unfortunate for you. I'll cut off your limbs and box them up. You won't even meet the black monk.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Senior.
As Shagal was surrounded by a mirage, numerous knives fell like hail.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Come at me.
The murderers were cautious of the inexplicable phenomenon, but retreat in hell meant eternal downfall.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: It's a joint attack.
As soon as he finished speaking, five murderers charged at Shagal.
The air trembled, and dozens of quick-blades moved as if alive.
Before Ethella could intervene, two murderers were downed, blades embedded throughout their bodies.
Aaaaah! The blood, the blood!
As all the blood spurted out, Shagal's face was again covered in blood.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Got you! Now!
The heavy Edron rolled agilely and caught Shagal's ankle with a steel cable.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: That's old-fashioned.
With a flick of his wrist holding the dagger, the quick-blade's shells repeatedly embedded themselves in Edron's back.
Ugh! Ugh! Ugh! Ugh!
As blood spouted like a fountain, Averio opened his cutter wide and advanced.

blocks.speaker: Averio
blocks.content: I'll start cutting from there!
The cutter moved towards the groin and snapped shut with the leverage of a lever.

blocks.speaker: Averio
blocks.content: Ugh! Ugh! Ugh! Ugh!
Averio's eyes flickered with confusion.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Hmph! Even so!
As he was about to crush even the iron, Shagal twisted his wrist 90 degrees and raised the dagger.
Aaaaah! My, my face!
The blade slashed across the jaw, and Shagal skillfully hacked him up.
Blades embedded in every vital point, Averio couldn't even fall as his body shook.
What? What? Aaaaah!
Suddenly, his eyes bulged and he burst like a balloon, spewing blood in all directions.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Senior? Just an old-fashioned style.
As Shagal turned his blood-soaked body, the face of the man holding the sickle scythe paled.

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: He's a monster. What the heck is that?
A murderer who terrorized an entire city was supposed to be skillful.

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: That guy isn't just any criminal. He's at a national, perhaps even kingdom-level. Maybe even...
Worldwide pursuit seemed plausible.

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: I don't understand. How could such a murderer be chained by fate's chains?
Wouldn't such a murderer belong in blade hell or cauldron hell?

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: Is that woman that important then?
As Shagal approached, the sickle man aimed his weapon at Ethella.

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: Stop! Come any closer, and I kill the woman.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Kill her. If you can.

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: I'm not joking! I'll really kill her! If this woman dies, you won't be safe either!

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Then kill her. I don't care.

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: You...!
As the sickle man turned quickly, Ethella leaped forward.
Ugh!
A flurry of punches from the Yin-Yang Wave struck the man's stomach, spilling blood.
Ugh!
Clutching his painful stomach, the man groaned as Shagal stepped on his back.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Start talking. Tell me everything you know if you don't want to end up like those seniors.

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: Ow... Ow...
The man could only nod repeatedly.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Hmm, black monk.
Having gained much information from the sickle man, Shagal and Ethella looked serious.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Let's summarize.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: There's an agency called the Hell Fire Management Corporation that manages hell. Humans who fall here are purified by hell fire and reborn as new demons.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Purified demons can be killed. But those like these guys who haven't entered the hell fire, meaning escapees, aren't considered dead even if killed.
Seeing Averio writhe as if purification might be better,

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Yes. And the entity that captures these escapees and purifies them is called the Black Monk (Black Chain). Perhaps they are akin to a Grim Reaper?

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Hey, have you actually seen a 'Black Monk' before?

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: Yes, I haven't been caught myself, but I've seen escapees being captured. They are dreadful beings, filled with a dark and damp aura. We need to move quickly. The Black Monks might come here.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: What do they look like?

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: So, they're dark and damp in aura...
As Shagal silently raised his quick-blade, the man made a face as if wronged,

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: Really. I don't even dare go near them to know exactly what they look like. When you see a Black Monk, you just run the other way. Please believe me.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Okay, moving on...

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: The Fire Management Company. You said they're watching us from the karma management department?

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: That's what I've heard.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Can they also respond if we ask how much time we have left to serve our penance?

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: Yes, I've seen that myself. Well, heard it, I suppose. Anyway, it's better not to hear it—it's an immense amount of time. One I saw was mad, randomly killing, wandering around for 1,200 years. I don't know what's become of him now. Well, he probably hasn't been purified yet. People who still have karma remaining, like us, can neither live nor die.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: How about you try? How much time do we have left together?
Desperate people dig wells, so Ethella looked up to the sky, hoping for a bearable amount of time.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: Are you watching us now? How much time do we have left until our duties are complete?
Time remaining until purification is...
a bureaucratic voice came from the sky.

blocks.speaker: Bureaucratic voice
blocks.content: 87 epochs, 2,875 eras, 3,241 trillion, 6,442 billion, 890 million, 7,109 hours remain.
As no one spoke, the man with the sickle scythe murmured with a dazed expression,

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: What, what? What kind of time is that?
Even though it wasn't his own karma, the time was so despairingly vast it brought him to tears.

blocks.speaker: Ethella
blocks.content: This can't be. How can such a time even be possible? What have I done to deserve this?
For your reference, your purification time is the seventh longest in the history of the underworld.
Lucifer held the record.

blocks.speaker: Informer Leona
blocks.content: Thank you for your intense emotions, and rest assured, the karma you've sent will be put to good use. Always striving, this is Informer Leona from the Fire Management Company.

blocks.speaker: Shagal
blocks.content: Haha, interesting. Take it slow then. We've got an eternity to spend together, right?
As Ethella was stunned, the man with the sickle looked at them and asked,

blocks.speaker: Sickle man
blocks.content: What on earth did you two do?
Tormia Royal Castle
Shirone, who was serving as the headquarters for simultaneous events, closed his book and rubbed the bridge of his nose.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The outside world.
Among the many events happening around the world, this was particularly shocking.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: OOPArts exist in material form. But when used, even humans become objects. They were never meant for human use.
The high-dimensional nature of the outside world was palpable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The problem is Ymir's mind. It's still not connected. I don't know what's happening.
The object Zhuangzi's Butterfly had clearly combined Shirone's information with signals from the outside world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In other words, the realm has changed. If that's the difference from the Gyorgi's Taiji or the Maika ruins…
The likelihood was high that all answers lay with Shirone, who was fighting within Ymir's mind.
At that moment, there was a knock on the door.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: Shirone.
Opening the door to the familiar voice, he saw Zulu, still pale, standing there.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Zulu, are you okay to be up?
Having suffered a critical injury from Ymir's attack, she had only regained consciousness three days ago.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: I'm recovering quickly. Still not ready for battle, but I wanted to hear what's been happening.
Please, come in.
Handing her a warm cup of tea, Shirone sat down with Zulu and recounted the recent events.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: Listening calmly to the many incidents, she raised her head at the mention of OOPArts.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: An ultra-ancient civilization, huh. You think it's connected to the outside world?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. I'm not certain. The Maika ruins are completely scattered, and the quantum signals I understood have changed, so they don't overlap.
Hmm.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: Shirone, would you like to go somewhere with me?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course, simultaneous events are always possible, but… you're still recovering.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: The Middle East, to Paras.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Paras? Are you visiting your hometown?
Setting down her teacup, Zulu conveyed her meaning precisely in her native language.

blocks.speaker: Zulu
blocks.content: There is an ultra-ancient civilization there.
